# API Security Data UX Checklist: Submit Feedback or Bug Report

**Purpose**: Validate the quality, clarity, and completeness of UC-19 requirements across API, security, data, and UX concerns before implementation.
**Created**: 2026-03-14
**Feature**: [spec.md](/home/asiad/ece493/311-forecast-system/specs/019-feedback-bug-reporting/spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## API Requirements Quality

- [x] CHK001 Are API surfaces for submission intake and reviewer retrieval both explicitly specified, including their distinct responsibilities? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001, Spec §FR-010, Plan §API Contract Direction]
- [x] CHK002 Are response outcome classes defined clearly for validation failure, accepted submission, delayed processing, and terminal failure outcomes? [Completeness, Spec §FR-003, Spec §FR-004, Spec §FR-007, Spec §User Story 3, Contract §paths]
- [x] CHK003 Are API authorization boundaries unambiguous between public submission endpoints and reviewer-only retrieval endpoints? [Clarity, Spec §FR-001, Spec §FR-010, Plan §Security model, Contract §security]
- [x] CHK004 Are request-field constraints (required report type, required description, optional contact) defined consistently between spec and API contract language? [Consistency, Spec §FR-002, Spec §FR-011, Spec §FR-012, Contract §FeedbackSubmissionCreateRequest]
- [X] CHK005 Are requirements explicit about unsupported or malformed request handling so teams do not infer divergent error formats? [Gap, Spec §FR-004, Plan §Implementation Notes, Contract §400 responses]
- [X] CHK006 Is lifecycle-status vocabulary (`accepted`, `deferred_for_retry`, `forwarded`, `forward_failed`) defined as canonical across API payloads and requirements? [Consistency, Plan §Phase 0 Research Decisions, Plan §API Contract Direction, Data Model §Canonical Vocabularies, Contract §ProcessingStatus]

## Security Requirements Quality

- [x] CHK007 Are authentication requirements specified clearly enough to allow anonymous submission while preventing anonymous reviewer access? [Coverage, Spec §Clarifications, Spec §FR-001, Spec §FR-010, Plan §Constitution Check]
- [x] CHK008 Are authorization expectations defined for who can read submission details, status history, and review queue records? [Completeness, Spec §FR-010, Plan §Service Direction, Data Model §ReviewQueueRecord]
- [X] CHK009 Are sensitive-data handling requirements explicit for optional contact details in storage, logs, and reviewer-facing responses? [Gap, Spec §FR-011, Spec §FR-009, Data Model §FeedbackSubmission]
- [x] CHK010 Are security failure outcomes documented separately from business-processing outcomes so unauthorized access is not conflated with submission-processing failures? [Clarity, Spec §FR-007, Plan §API Contract Direction, Contract §401/403 responses]
- [X] CHK011 Are dependency assumptions about external issue-tracking trust boundaries and credential handling documented as requirements rather than implicit implementation knowledge? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions & Dependencies, Plan §Technical Context]
- [X] CHK012 Are auditability requirements defined for security-relevant reviewer actions (read/list) or intentionally excluded with rationale? [Gap, Spec §FR-009, Plan §Post-Design Constitution Check]

## Data Requirements Quality

- [x] CHK013 Are all required entities for UC-19 intake and review (`FeedbackSubmission`, `SubmissionStatusEvent`, `ReviewQueueRecord`) fully defined with purpose and relationships? [Completeness, Spec §Key Entities, Plan §Data Model Direction, Data Model §Relationships]
- [x] CHK014 Are identity and uniqueness expectations specified for accepted submissions and status events to prevent ambiguous traceability? [Clarity, Data Model §FeedbackSubmission, Data Model §SubmissionStatusEvent]
- [X] CHK015 Are state-transition expectations for processing statuses and triage statuses documented clearly enough to avoid conflicting workflows? [Consistency, Plan §Phase 0 Research Decisions, Data Model §Canonical Vocabularies, Data Model §ReviewQueueRecord]
- [X] CHK016 Are data-retention expectations for submissions and status history defined or explicitly scoped out? [Gap, Spec §SC-004, Data Model §SubmissionStatusEvent]
- [X] CHK017 Are duplicate-submission handling requirements defined clearly as capture-separately behavior rather than implicit deduplication? [Clarity, Spec §Edge Cases, Research §Keep duplicate submissions as separate records]
- [x] CHK018 Are assumptions about eventual retry processing sufficiently specified to make deferred submissions verifiable and reviewable? [Dependency, Spec §Assumptions & Dependencies, Spec §FR-008, Plan §FeedbackRetryService]

## UX Requirements Quality

- [x] CHK019 Are user-facing requirements complete for successful submission, validation-error correction, delayed-processing notice, and failure notice states? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §FR-007]
- [X] CHK020 Are validation-message requirements specific enough that “clear validation errors” is objectively interpretable across required fields? [Clarity, Spec §FR-004, Spec §Acceptance Scenarios (User Story 2)]
- [x] CHK021 Is the required report-type selection UX explicitly defined enough to prevent inconsistent treatment of missing type across channels? [Consistency, Spec §FR-012, Spec §Acceptance Scenario 2 (User Story 1)]
- [x] CHK022 Are requirements defined for what users see when external forwarding is unavailable but local acceptance succeeds? [Completeness, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §FR-008, Spec §SC-002]
- [X] CHK023 Are edge UX requirements specified for abandoned forms, rapid duplicate submissions, and oversized descriptions, or are these intentionally deferred? [Edge Case, Gap, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [X] CHK024 Are accessibility expectations for form fields, validation feedback, and submission outcomes defined or explicitly excluded? [Gap, Non-Functional, Spec §User Scenarios & Testing]

## Cross-Domain Consistency & Measurability

- [x] CHK025 Are success criteria measurable and aligned with API, data, and UX requirements without requiring implementation-specific interpretation? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001 through §SC-004]
- [X] CHK026 Are there any conflicts between the spec’s statement that accepted submissions are retained and the contract/status vocabulary used to represent forwarding failures? [Conflict, Spec §FR-008, Plan §Implementation Notes, Contract §ProcessingStatus]
- [X] CHK027 Are dependency assumptions (external tracker availability, reviewer team existence, retry operations) all linked to explicit requirement text rather than only plan narrative? [Dependency, Spec §Assumptions & Dependencies, Plan §Summary]
- [X] CHK028 Are terms such as “accepted,” “stored,” “recorded for review,” and “forwarded” used consistently enough across spec, plan, data model, and contract to avoid ambiguous acceptance interpretation? [Consistency, Spec §FR-005 through §FR-010, Plan §Data Model Direction, Data Model §Overview, Contract §schemas]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline.
- This consolidated checklist intentionally covers API, security, data, and UX requirement quality only (performance replaced by UX for UC-19).
